pattern_description: SAP PM work orders geo-coded via ArcGIS for field crew routing and asset location context. Asset IDs in SAP PM linked to GIS feature IDs enabling location-aware work order display on dispatcher map.

GE ADMS automatically generates trouble orders in Oracle Utilities Work Management upon detection of confirmed outages. Restoration confirmation events flow back from Oracle WM to update OMS outage record status.

ArcGIS serves as the system of record for asset geography. Salesforce FSM pulls asset location, network connectivity, and asset attributes to provide field crew with GIS context on the mobile app. Work completion data writes back to GIS for asset record updates.

IBM Maximo EAM integrated with ArcGIS to link work orders to GIS asset features. Asset inspection work orders include GIS coordinates and network location. Completion data updates asset inspection dates in GIS.

Itron AMI outage pings (last gasp messages from smart meters) are ingested by GE ADMS as outage detection signals, reducing SAIDI by accelerating confirmed outage identification before customer calls arrive.

Migration integration pattern — utilities migrating from IBM Maximo to SAP EAM/PM synchronize work order history, asset records, and job plans during transition period. Both systems run in parallel during cutover.

Salesforce FSM used as the scheduling and mobile dispatch layer while SAP PM remains the system of record for work orders and asset history. Work orders created in SAP PM flow to Salesforce for scheduling; completion records flow back to SAP.

AVEVA PI historian monitors asset health time-series data (transformer temperatures, breaker operation counts). When thresholds are exceeded, PI triggers predictive maintenance work orders in IBM Maximo for proactive field intervention.
GE ADMS generates outage trouble orders that are dispatched through Salesforce FSM for crew assignment and routing. SAIDI restoration timestamps written back to ADMS upon crew completion.

Oracle Utilities WM integrated with ArcGIS for work location geo-coding and asset context. Field crew see the network map with outage polygon overlay and asset attributes pulled from ArcGIS on mobile device.

MuleSoft serves as the integration hub for Salesforce FSM, connecting it to OMS (GE ADMS or Oracle Utilities), GIS (ArcGIS), ERP (SAP or Oracle), and AMI (Itron/Landis+Gyr). Single API layer reduces point-to-point integration sprawl.

GE Smallworld provides the asset geography and network model; SAP PM holds the work order and maintenance history. Integration enables work orders in SAP PM to be displayed on Smallworld maps and GIS asset IDs linked to SAP equipment master records.
Landis+Gyr smart meter last-gasp outage pings fed into Milsoft OMS for smaller utilities and cooperatives. Accelerates outage detection without requiring the OMS to wait for customer calls.

ServiceNow FSM used for IT/OT convergence use cases — managing both IT infrastructure field work and physical asset maintenance in a unified platform, with work orders synchronized from SAP PM for OT assets.

IBM Maximo functions as the EAM system of record for asset master data and maintenance history. Salesforce FSM provides scheduling optimization and mobile field worker capabilities. Work orders created in Maximo are scheduled and dispatched via Salesforce; completion data flows back.
